title: fraxinus pennsylvanica var. ovata
intro: Fraxinus pennsylvanica var. ovata, also known as the Green Ash, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its compound leaves and greenish-gray bark. The bark and leaves of the Green Ash have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus pennsylvanica var. ovata include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the Green Ash include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies.
